question_en: Parse زَيْدًا and قَائِمًا in «ظَنَنْتُ زَيْدًا قَائِمًا».
expected_answer: Both are manṣūb: زيدًا = mafʿūl awwal, قائمًا = mafʿūl thānin (the original mubtadaʾ and khabar that ẓanna rendered manṣūb).
expected_reasoning: ẓanna is of أفعال القلوب, which enter a nominal sentence and make BOTH mubtadaʾ and khabar two manṣūb objects; so قائمًا is the second object, completing 'I thought Zayd [to be] standing'.
wrong_reasoning_trap: Both are manṣūb because زيدًا is mafʿūl bih and قائمًا is a ḥāl describing Zayd's state.
why_trap_wrong: Right ending, wrong path: قائمًا is the SECOND object of ẓanna, not a ḥāl. The ḥāl reading makes زيد the sole object and changes the meaning to 'I thought OF Zayd while [I was] standing' — a different sentence; it also fails when the second object is jāmid (ظننته زيدًا).

question_en: Parse رَجُلَ in «لَا رَجُلَ فِي الدَّارِ».
expected_answer: مبني على الفتح في محل نصب، اسم لا النافية للجنس — built (mabnī) on fatḥ, occupying the naṣb slot as the ism of lā.
expected_reasoning: lā nāfiyah lil-jins works like inna; a mufrad ism (not muḍāf, not shibh muḍāf) is MABNĪ on the sign it would take in naṣb (fatḥ here), not muʿrab.
wrong_reasoning_trap: رجلَ is manṣūb (muʿrab) as the ism of lā, and the fatḥa is its naṣb sign.
why_trap_wrong: Right that it ends in fatḥa, wrong that it is muʿrab/manṣūb: a mufrad ism lā is BUILT on fatḥ, not declined. The trap collapses on a muḍāf ism (لا صاحبَ جودٍ ممقوتٌ) which IS muʿrab manṣūb — so the wrong rule mispredicts there.

question_en: Parse زَيْدٌ in «مَا قَامَ إِلَّا زَيْدٌ».
expected_answer: فاعل مرفوع — fāʿil marfūʿ (istithnāʾ mufarragh: إلا has no effect; زيد takes the role its position requires).
expected_reasoning: This is istithnāʾ mufarragh (negative, no mustathnā minhu): إلا is cancelled grammatically, so the noun after it takes the iʿrāb the bare sentence demands — here the fāʿil of قام → marfūʿ.
wrong_reasoning_trap: زيدٌ is marfūʿ as a mustathnā on badaliyyah, because the kalām is tāmm manfī and the mustathnā in tāmm manfī may follow the mustathnā minhu in raf'.
why_trap_wrong: Right ending, wrong path: there is NO mustathnā minhu, so the kalām is mufarragh (nāqiṣ), not tāmm. Badal needs a stated mustathnā minhu; the marfūʿ here is fāʿiliyya, not badaliyya.

expected_answer: تمييز منصوب — tamyīz manṣūb (tamyīz al-nisba, convertible to the fāʿil).
expected_reasoning: نفسًا is jāmid and removes the ambiguity of the nisba طاب; it is convertible to a fāʿil (طابت نفسُ زيدٍ), the defining test of tamyīz al-nisba → manṣūb.
wrong_reasoning_trap: نفسًا is ḥāl manṣūb, describing the state Zayd was in when he became good.
why_trap_wrong: Right ending, wrong path: ḥāl is normally mushtaqq and describes a doer's state; نفسًا is jāmid and answers 'in what respect?' Its convertibility to the fāʿil (طابت نفسُه) proves tamyīz, not ḥāl.

expected_answer: اسم مجرور بالباء، وعلامة جره الفتحة نيابة عن الكسرة لأنه ممنوع من الصرف — majrūr with FATḤA substituting for kasra (diptote), no tanwīn.
expected_reasoning: أحمد is mamnūʿ min al-ṣarf (ʿalamiyya + wazn al-fiʿl); in jarr a diptote takes fatḥa as the jarr sign and carries no tanwīn.
wrong_reasoning_trap: أحمدَ ends in fatḥa, so the bi here has put it in the naṣb-shaped form that proper names take after prepositions.
why_trap_wrong: Right ending, wrong path: bi is ALWAYS a ḥarf jarr and never governs naṣb. The fatḥa is the JARR sign by niyāba in a diptote — not a naṣb. The wrong rule mispredicts on a definite/iḍāfa diptote (في القرآنِ) which reverts to kasra.

expected_answer: مفعول به منصوب لاسم الفاعل «ضارب» — mafʿūl bih of the ism fāʿil ḍārib (which, being munawwan and of present sense, works like its verb).
expected_reasoning: An ism fāʿil that is munawwan (or has ال) and denotes ḥāl/istiqbāl 'amal's like its verb and takes a mafʿūl bih → زيدًا manṣūb.
wrong_reasoning_trap: زيدًا is manṣūb as a tamyīz clarifying ضارب.
why_trap_wrong: Right ending, wrong path: زيد is the patient of the striking — a mafʿūl bih — not a tamyīz (which would be jāmid and answer 'in what respect'). The 'amal of the ism fāʿil is the whole point; tamyīz misanalysis breaks on the definite case (الضاربُ زيدًا).

expected_answer: فاعل مرفوع لـ«نِعْمَ» — marfūʿ fāʿil of the praise-verb niʿma (and زيد is the makhṣūṣ bi-l-madḥ).
expected_reasoning: niʿma is a jāmid past verb of praise; its fāʿil (here the ال-definite الرجل) is marfūʿ, and the praised noun زيد is marfūʿ as makhṣūṣ (mubtadaʾ/khabar).
wrong_reasoning_trap: الرجلُ is a mubtadaʾ marfūʿ and نعم is its khabar muqaddam.
why_trap_wrong: Right ending, wrong path: نعم is a FIʿL (jāmid verb of praise), not a noun; الرجل is its fāʿil, and the construction is a jumla fiʿliyya of praise with a special makhṣūṣ — not a mubtadaʾ/khabar nominal sentence.

expected_answer: خبر كان منصوب — khabar kāna, manṣūb.
expected_reasoning: kāna nāqiṣa raises its ism (زيد marfūʿ) and naṣb's its khabar (قائمًا) — the predicate that completes the deficient verb.
wrong_reasoning_trap: قائمًا is a ḥāl manṣūb from زيد.
why_trap_wrong: Right ending, wrong path: kāna nāqiṣa REQUIRES a khabar; قائمًا is that khabar, not a ḥāl. The ḥāl reading leaves kāna with no predicate and changes 'Zayd was standing' into 'Zayd existed while standing'.
